title: Procedural City Builder with Avatar
prompt:
Create a single HTML file with a procedural city builder using Three.js. Load the avatar model 'Xbot.glb' using GLTFLoader from a CDN. The scene must implement the following:

## Scene Setup
- Dark ground plane (100x100, color #2a2a2a) at y=0.
- Directional light at (50, 80, 50) with intensity 1.2, casting shadows.
- Ambient light with intensity 0.4.
- Perspective camera starting at position (30, 40, 30) looking at origin.
- OrbitControls for camera manipulation.
- Sky-blue background (#87CEEB).

## City Grid System
- The city is built on a 10x10 grid (each cell is 5x5 units).
- Grid lines should be visible on the ground plane (thin lines, color #444444).
- Grid cells are indexed [row][col] from 0-9.

## Building Types (placed as BoxGeometry)
- 'residential': color #4CAF50, width 4, depth 4, random height 5-15
- 'commercial': color #2196F3, width 4, depth 4, random height 10-25
- 'industrial': color #FF9800, width 4, depth 4, height 8 (fixed)
- 'park': a flat green plane (color #66BB6A) with 3 small CylinderGeometry trees (brown trunk + green sphere canopy)

## Avatar (Xbot.glb)
- Load Xbot.glb and place it at grid cell [5][5] (world position center of that cell).
- Scale the avatar to 3 units tall.
- The avatar represents the city inspector.
- Use WASD keys to move the avatar one grid cell at a time (discrete movement, animated lerp over 300ms).
- The avatar should face the direction of movement (rotate Y accordingly: W=north/-Z, S=south/+Z, A=west/-X, D=east/+X).
- Clamp avatar to grid bounds (0-9 in both axes).

## Building Placement
- A HUD panel at top-left with 4 buttons: 'Residential', 'Commercial', 'Industrial', 'Park'.
- Clicking a button sets the current building type (highlight selected button with a border).
- Press SPACE to place the selected building type at the avatar's current grid cell.
- Cannot place a building on an already occupied cell (show brief red flash on the button panel).
- Each placed building increments the building count.

## Demolish Mode
- A 'Demolish' toggle button in the HUD. When active, pressing SPACE removes the building at the avatar's current cell.
- Demolish button should turn red (#f44336) when active.

## Population & Economy HUD
- Display at top-right: Population, Revenue, Buildings count.
- Each residential building adds 100 population.
- Each commercial building adds 500 revenue.
- Each industrial building adds 200 revenue but -10 population.
- Parks add 50 population (happiness bonus).
- Update these values in real-time when buildings are placed or demolished.

## Day/Night Cycle
- A 'Toggle Day/Night' button. Night mode: background #1a1a3e, directional light intensity 0.3, ambient 0.15. Day mode restores defaults.
- Buildings in night mode should have small emissive glow (emissive property).

## State Management (window.__3D_STATE__)
Expose the following on window.__3D_STATE__:
- `gridState`: 2D array [10][10], each cell null or {type: string} 
- `avatarGridPos`: {row: number, col: number}
- `avatarWorldPos`: {x: number, y: number, z: number}
- `selectedBuildingType`: string ('residential'|'commercial'|'industrial'|'park'|null)
- `demolishMode`: boolean
- `buildingCount`: number
- `population`: number
- `revenue`: number
- `isNight`: boolean
- `avatarLoaded`: boolean
- `totalResidential`: number
- `totalCommercial`: number
- `totalIndustrial`: number
- `totalParks`: number

Update __3D_STATE__ on every relevant change. Ensure all numerical values are never NaN.
physics_constraints: Avatar movement is grid-locked (discrete cells). Buildings snap to grid centers. Avatar cannot move outside 0-9 grid bounds. Population and revenue follow additive rules per building type. No two buildings can occupy the same cell.
